entities.description: A cereal crop discussed in the context of seedling survival under abiotic stress

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop discussed in the context of seedling survival under abiotic stress

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Non-living environmental factors that negatively impact plant growth and survival

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing crops for food or other purposes

entities.name: ARID AND SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Geographic regions characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures

entities.name: HIGH TEMPERATURES
entities.description: Elevated thermal conditions that contribute to abiotic stress

entities.name: HIGH EVAPORATIVE DEMAND
entities.description: Environmental condition leading to rapid water loss from soil and plants

entities.name: UNRELIABLE AND IRREGULAR RAINFALL
entities.description: Inconsistent precipitation patterns affecting water availability

entities.name: POOR SOIL STRUCTURE
entities.description: Soils characterized by low fertility and low water-holding capacity

entities.name: SEEDBED PREPARATION
entities.description: The process of preparing the soil for sowing seeds

entities.name: SOWING METHODS
entities.description: Techniques used for planting seeds in the soil

entities.name: SEEDLING GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The speed at which a seedling grows, which can be reduced by abiotic stress

entities.name: MORTALITY
entities.description: The death of seedlings during germination or emergence

entities.name: GERMINATION
entities.description: The process by which a plant grows from a seed

entities.name: SEEDLING EMERGENCE
entities.description: The process of a seedling breaking through the soil surface

entities.name: STAND FAILURE
entities.description: The failure of a crop to establish a sufficient population of plants

entities.name: SEEDLING STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a seedling to survive and grow under abiotic stress

entities.name: SCREENING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used to identify plants with desirable traits

entities.name: POPULATION IMPROVEMENT PROGRAMS
entities.description: Systematic efforts to improve the genetic quality of a crop population

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKERS
entities.description: DNA sequences used to identify specific genes or traits

entities.name: THERMOTOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand high temperatures

entities.name: GENETIC IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of altering the genetic makeup of a crop to improve traits

entities.name: AGRONOMIC PRACTICE
entities.description: Management techniques used in crop production
